query_normalized: the impact of perspective-taking on moral outrage in online discourse psychology
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by measuring the same core mechanism (perspective-taking via a virtual agent) and the same emotional antecedent (anger) as the user's question, even though the dependent variable is creative problem-solving performance rather than moral outrage. It also fits criterion (b) by providing empirical evidence on how perspective-taking interventions modulate the effects of anger in an online experimental setting, which is a canonical variable and domain for the user's
